jokes.text: Jeff's non-response when asked if Larry talked during the putt, followed by Larry's 'That's everything! He just said everything!'
jokes.setup: Larry asks Jeff to settle a golf dispute about talking during a putt
jokes.punchline: Jeff's strategic silence reveals the answer, which Larry immediately recognizes
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David paranoia meeting Jeff's conflict avoidance. Perfect character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Larry quotes Dante about the hottest places in hell being for those who retain neutrality in times of crisis - over a golf putt
jokes.setup: Jeff won't take sides in a minor golf dispute
jokes.punchline: Larry invokes Dante's Inferno over something completely trivial
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Larry's tendency to escalate minor grievances into cosmic moral issues. The literary pretension makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: The revelation that Hal slept with his mother-in-law, delivered as club gossip
jokes.setup: Larry wonders why Hal is avoiding him after a date setup
jokes.punchline: The bombshell about Hal's sexual history that explains everything
jokes.explanation: Classic Curb escalation - a minor social issue has a shocking sexual scandal underneath.

jokes.text: Larry's direct question: 'Did you fuck your mother-in-law at any point? 'Cause I ain't setting you up if you did.'
jokes.setup: Larry has just learned about Hal's scandal and Jeff wants to be set up
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt, practical approach to vetting potential dates
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David directness - he addresses the elephant in the room with zero tact but total practicality.

jokes.text: Did you fuck your mother-in-law at any point? 'Cause I ain't setting you up if you did.
jokes.setup: Larry about to set up Jeff with Gabby
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt screening question treating mother-in-law fucking as a dating disqualifier
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic - treating extreme taboo as routine dating vetting. Perfect character voice and economy.

jokes.text: The heart attack scene with Larry coaching: 'Squeeze as hard as hard as you can! Okay...' followed by awkward singing
jokes.setup: Saul is having a heart attack and Larry is trying to help
jokes.punchline: Larry's awkward, inexperienced attempts at emergency care including spontaneous singing
jokes.explanation: Larry's discomfort with emotional/medical situations creates authentic cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Jeff's comment about T-shirt exposure: 'You can't have more than three-quarters of an inch of T-shirt showing.'
jokes.setup: Jeff's shirt button fell off before his date
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific measurement for acceptable undershirt exposure
jokes.explanation: Captures male vanity and arbitrary fashion rules with specificity.

jokes.text: Freddy burning himself and Larry's response: 'Oh, Jesus. I just burned the shit out of my hand.' 'What? Oh, jeez.' 'Because I just found something pretty hot.'
jokes.setup: Freddy appears to burn himself looking through Larry's closet
jokes.punchline: The 'burn' was metaphorical - he found an attractive shirt
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection pun, competently executed but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Larry's confusion about the weather: 'I thought it was gonna rain. What happened?' 'They said rain.' 'I'm not crazy.'
jokes.setup: Larry brought an umbrella but it's not raining
jokes.punchline: His defensive reaction to carrying an umbrella unnecessarily
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry insecurity about looking foolish, defending rational but wrong decisions.

jokes.text: Ohitorisama goraiten desu! Irasshaimase!
jokes.setup: Larry enters Japanese restaurant alone
jokes.punchline: Elaborate formal Japanese greeting for single diner
jokes.explanation: Sets up the central running gag about Japanese restaurant etiquette and irasshaimase.

jokes.text: The enthusiastic 'Irasshaimase!' greeting and Larry's reaction: 'Oh! Well. Thank you, thank you. I love that greeting.'
jokes.setup: Larry receives an enthusiastic Japanese greeting
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top appreciation sets up the coming conflict
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the episode's central running gag. Larry's enthusiasm makes the payoff stronger.

jokes.text: Gabby's claim: 'Oh, yeah. I speak six languages, including Guamish. That's what they speak in Guam.'
jokes.setup: Larry asks if she speaks Japanese
jokes.punchline: She claims to speak a made-up language from Guam
jokes.explanation: Establishes Gabby as pretentious and possibly full of shit. 'Guamish' is a nice comic invention.

jokes.text: The chef's correction: 'Could you please not say "irasshaimase"?' followed by the explanation about sushi chefs vs. guests
jokes.setup: Larry enthusiastically uses the Japanese greeting
jokes.punchline: He's violating an etiquette rule he didn't know existed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David situation - cultural ignorance meets rigid rules. Sets up the episode's main conflict.

jokes.text: Larry's indignant response: 'I have been here 100 times. I've never been reprimanded by the host.'
jokes.setup: Larry is corrected about saying 'irasshaimase'
jokes.punchline: He treats it like a serious violation of his customer rights
jokes.explanation: Larry's sense of injustice over minor social corrections is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Alas, no.
jokes.setup: Gabby asks if this is his first reprimand
jokes.punchline: Larry's pretentious 'alas' admission of multiple reprimands
jokes.explanation: Perfect word choice showing Larry's pretension while admitting social failures. Great economy.

jokes.text: Jeff's dating advice about Larry: 'Worst thing that happens, it doesn't work out, he's a great person to break up with.'
jokes.setup: Gabby explains why she agreed to the date
jokes.punchline: Jeff's backhanded compliment about Larry's emotional detachment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Larry's emotional unavailability as both a flaw and oddly practical feature.

jokes.text: Larry's breakup process: 'All you gotta do is say, "Hey, I don't wanna see you anymore." And I go, "Ah! Okay." Not another thought.'
jokes.setup: Larry explains his easy breakup process
jokes.punchline: His complete emotional detachment presented as a selling point
jokes.explanation: Larry's sociopathic tendencies presented as relationship benefits. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Gabby's assessment: 'That's almost, um... like a sociopath, but... borderline.' Larry: 'You know what? That's one of the nicest things anyone's ever said to me.'
jokes.setup: Gabby calls Larry borderline sociopathic
jokes.punchline: Larry takes it as a genuine compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Larry's twisted psychology on full display. The sincerity of his gratitude is hilarious.

jokes.text: Gabby's secret: 'When I was younger, I was a teacher in high school... and I slept with one of my students.'
jokes.setup: They're sharing secrets on their first date
jokes.punchline: Her secret is statutory rape presented casually
jokes.explanation: Shocking revelation that escalates the date's inappropriate intimacy level.

jokes.text: Larry's follow-up questions: 'Did you go to the prom?' 'Did you go to the games? Did he give you a little wink when he was playing?'
jokes.setup: Gabby reveals her inappropriate relationship with a student
jokes.punchline: Larry focuses on the mundane logistics rather than the ethical implications
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry David moment - completely missing the moral issue while fixating on amusing details.

jokes.text: Larry's big secret: 'I have a fantastic shortcut to the valley.'
jokes.setup: After hearing Gabby's shocking confession, Larry promises an equally big secret
jokes.punchline: His 'secret' is completely mundane traffic information
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax. The massive expectation vs. reality gap is expertly constructed and quintessentially Larry.

jokes.text: Gabby's reaction: 'I told you... that I slept with a student. Your secret is Waze.'
jokes.setup: The massive disparity between their 'secrets'
jokes.punchline: Her reduction of Larry's shortcut to just being like a GPS app
jokes.explanation: Perfect punctuation of the joke. 'Your secret is Waze' is brutally efficient comedy writing.

jokes.text: Larry's defense: 'My secret improves your quality of life. Your secret's just interesting.'
jokes.setup: Gabby points out the disparity between their secrets
jokes.punchline: Larry's twisted logic that traffic tips are more valuable than shocking confessions
jokes.explanation: Larry's ability to rationalize anything is on full display. His practical focus is both logical and completely missing the point.

jokes.text: Larry wanting to write down the shortcut and Gabby's refusal: 'You lose it, somebody finds it. No good.'
jokes.setup: Larry wants to remember the shortcut route
jokes.punchline: Gabby treats the traffic route like classified information while her own secret was statutory rape
jokes.explanation: Nice continuation of the twisted priorities - treating mundane information as more sensitive than criminal activity.

jokes.text: Larry's inability to keep Gabby's secret, immediately telling Jeff she 'fucked one of her students'
jokes.setup: Larry promised to keep Gabby's secret and claimed to be discreet
jokes.punchline: He blabs it immediately to Jeff
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry hypocrisy - claiming discretion while being completely indiscreet. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: Larry's defense: 'I didn't directly tell him, I indirectly...' 'But you did!'
jokes.setup: Jeff calls out Larry for immediately breaking his promise
jokes.punchline: Larry tries to argue semantics about how he revealed the secret
jokes.explanation: Larry's legalistic approach to moral failures - trying to find technicalities to avoid responsibility.

jokes.text: The discussion about which high school teachers were attractive, ending with 'Miss Rogers was not the cause of any tumescence among the young men in my school'
jokes.setup: Discussing Gabby's student affair leads to reminiscing about school
jokes.punchline: Larry's pretentious use of 'tumescence' to describe teenage arousal
jokes.explanation: Larry's vocabulary pretensions make even crude topics sound academic. 'Tumescence' is a perfectly Larry word choice.

jokes.text: Description of Miss Rogers: 'She was on death's door. Veins popping out all over her body.'
jokes.setup: Explaining why Miss Rogers wasn't attractive
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely specific physical description
jokes.explanation: Mildly gross-out humor that's competent but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Every time I had a tummy ache, I tapped that ass.
jokes.setup: Leon claiming he had sex with school nurse
jokes.punchline: Crude description of fake sexual encounters
jokes.explanation: Leon's typical crude fantasy story, in character but relies on shock value.

jokes.text: Larry's polite dismissal: 'Thank you... Thank you for your contribution. He's got a wild imagination.'
jokes.setup: Freddy's over-the-top sexual story
jokes.punchline: Larry's formal, polite way of calling bullshit
jokes.explanation: Larry's diplomatic but cutting way of dismissing obvious lies. The formal tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Jeff's logic: 'If I tell you the shortcut, then it's no longer a shortcut.'
jokes.setup: Larry wants the shortcut information
jokes.punchline: Jeff's surprisingly sound reasoning about traffic patterns
jokes.explanation: Perfectly logical explanation that makes Larry's hoarding seem reasonable. Good reversal of expectations.

jokes.text: The shirt stain revelation and Larry's description: 'Looks like you were a part of a home birth or something.'
jokes.setup: Freddy has ruined Larry's favorite shirt
jokes.punchline: Larry's specific and gross comparison for the stain pattern
jokes.explanation: Vivid and disgusting comparison that perfectly captures the shirt's appearance. Good visual comedy.

jokes.text: Larry's revelation: 'I didn't know soap stains.' Freddy: 'Soap stains.' Larry: 'Who makes a cleaning product that stains? That's like a Band-Aid that cuts!'
jokes.setup: Freddy used bar soap to clean the shirt, making it worse
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical outrage about contradictory product functions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational comedy with perfect analogy. The Band-Aid comparison is inspired and quotable.

jokes.text: How do you even do it? Do you get on your knees? Do you put your hands together?
jokes.setup: Hal asking Larry to pray for his father
jokes.punchline: Larry's mechanical questions about prayer logistics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry atheism - treating prayer like technical instructions. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Larry putting himself in God's shoes: 'Oh, there's the son! Oh, the son's praying! Eh... I need more than that... Oh! Oh, there... Ah, Larry David! Oh! Okay, I'll save him!'
jokes.setup: Hal argues that Larry's prayer might be the thing that saves his father
jokes.punchline: Larry's egomaniacal fantasy about his special importance to God
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David megalomania - even his religious skepticism becomes about his own importance.

jokes.text: Larry's proof that prayers don't work: 'Because I'm bald.'
jokes.setup: Hal asks how Larry knows prayers don't work
jokes.punchline: Larry's personal evidence - his own baldness
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline - economical, personal, and completely Larry. The logic is both absurd and oddly convincing.

jokes.text: Hal's cryptic warning: 'What goes around comes around.'
jokes.setup: Larry asks what happened with Hal and Gabby
jokes.punchline: Hal's ominous non-explanation that hints at future consequences
jokes.explanation: Good setup for future consequences, though the phrase itself is cliche.

jokes.text: The question of lending favorite items: 'Why would you loan me your favorite shirt?' 'Because I'm a friend, and you were uncomfortable going on the date.' 'Still, you don't give out your favorite shirt. Everybody knows that.'
jokes.setup: The revelation that it was Freddy's favorite shirt
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredulity at Freddy's poor lending decisions
jokes.explanation: Good exploration of social rules around lending personal items. Larry's logic is sound but selfish.

jokes.text: Freddy's biblical justice demand: 'I think the thing that would... Would mean something to me would be you actually... giving me your favorite shirt.' Larry: 'That's really biblical, real eye-for-eye stuff.'
jokes.setup: Freddy wants compensation for his ruined favorite shirt
jokes.punchline: His demand for Larry's favorite shirt as equivalent exchange
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the shirt conflict with biblical reference adding gravitas to a trivial dispute.

jokes.text: Larry's refusal and Freddy's counter: 'You're not getting my blue and gray two-tone, okay? You don't give up a two-tone. That's my favorite shirt.'
jokes.setup: Freddy wants Larry's favorite shirt as compensation
jokes.punchline: Larry's protective stance over his two-tone shirt
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's hypocrisy - expecting generosity he won't give. The two-tone becomes a sacred object.

jokes.text: That's because when you say it, it sound redneck.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about being corrected for saying irasshaimase
jokes.punchline: Takahashi's blunt assessment of Larry's pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural observation about Larry's butchered pronunciation. Good callback development.

jokes.text: Mr. Takahashi's pronunciation critique: 'That's because when you say it, it sound redneck.' 'You know terrible? That's you.'
jokes.setup: Larry tries to learn proper pronunciation
jokes.punchline: Brutal assessment of Larry's linguistic abilities
jokes.explanation: Sharp insult with specific cultural context. 'That's you' is brutally economical.

jokes.text: Only plastic surgeon here.
jokes.setup: Larry noting lack of doctors during heart attack at country club
jokes.punchline: Country club only has cosmetic doctors, not life-saving ones
jokes.explanation: Sharp social satire about wealthy club priorities - appearance over substance.

jokes.text: The observation about the country club: 'It's interesting because you would think that having a heart attack at a country club like this, there'd be doctors around.' 'Only plastic surgeon here.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of Saul's death at the club
jokes.punchline: The country club only attracts cosmetic surgeons, not life-saving doctors
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about country club demographics and medical priorities. Good social commentary.

jokes.text: The umbrella standoff: 'Should I return it to you or Dallas Omni Hotel?' followed by the escalating argument about ownership and honor
jokes.setup: Larry tries to reclaim his borrowed umbrella
jokes.punchline: A philosophical battle over returning property to the rightful owner
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of a simple situation into philosophical debate. Both parties have valid points.

jokes.text: The honor battle: 'No, it's my honor!' 'My honor!' escalating to 'My honor! I want that umbrella! That's my umbrella!'
jokes.setup: The polite battle over who has the honor of returning the umbrella
jokes.punchline: Escalating politeness becomes increasingly frantic and desperate
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from courtesy to mania. Larry's desperation breaking through polite facade is great character work.

jokes.text: The manager's comeback: 'A bald man steal umbrella! Yes, let's report!'
jokes.setup: Larry threatens to call police about umbrella theft
jokes.punchline: Manager turns it around, making Larry the criminal and referencing his baldness
jokes.explanation: Good reversal making Larry the bad guy, plus nice callback to the baldness theme.

jokes.text: Larry's final irasshaimase rant: 'I'll irasshaimase any time I want! I like irasshaimaseing! It's very welcoming! It feels very good to irasshaimase, and I'll continue to irasshaimase!'
jokes.setup: The entire episode's worth of irasshaimase conflicts
jokes.punchline: Larry's defiant declaration of linguistic independence
jokes.explanation: Perfect climax to the episode's main running gag. Larry's rebellious stance is both childish and principled.

jokes.text: Larry's defense: 'I was here literally, but not figuratively.'
jokes.setup: Cheryl blames him for not answering her door
jokes.punchline: Larry's philosophical distinction about presence
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry legalistic logic to avoid responsibility. The literal/figurative distinction is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Cheryl's comeback: 'Oh! No shit, Sherlock! Just get the fucking dress, okay?'
jokes.setup: Larry quotes Sherlock Holmes while giving relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's sarcastic reference to the detective show he was watching
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the TV scene with efficient put-down. 'No shit, Sherlock' is both literal and figurative.

jokes.text: Larry's traffic philosophy: 'I'm too smart. I'm not like these people. You have to have done something stupid to be in traffic. I don't belong here.'
jokes.setup: Larry is stuck in traffic on his way to the funeral
jokes.punchline: His elitist attitude about traffic intelligence while being stuck himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry arrogance - he considers himself above traffic while being in it. The irony is delicious.

jokes.text: Freddy's funeral speech calling out Larry: 'Larry David, uh, couldn't make it here today. He got stuck in traffic, and he turned around and went home.'
jokes.setup: Freddy is giving a eulogy about friendship and sacrifice
jokes.punchline: He publicly shames Larry for missing the funeral
jokes.explanation: Devastating public shaming at a funeral. Freddy's passive-aggressive revenge is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: The shirt metaphor in the eulogy: 'Saul was the kind of guy that would give you the shirt off his back, even if it was his favorite one... And if he was the one to stain that shirt? Guess what. He would've given you his favorite shirt back.'
jokes.setup: Freddy is eulogizing Saul's generosity
jokes.punchline: He's actually talking about Larry and the shirt incident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant use of funeral eulogy as passive-aggressive attack. The metaphor is obvious to everyone.

jokes.text: He would've given you his favorite shirt back.
jokes.setup: Freddy praising the deceased's generosity
jokes.punchline: Pointed contrast with Larry's shirt replacement refusal
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback using eulogy context to shame Larry publicly for his pettiness.

jokes.text: Larry's deduction about Gabby and Hal: 'She must've asked for a secret on their date... then told everybody about the mother-in-law.'
jokes.setup: Larry figures out what went wrong with Hal and Gabby
jokes.punchline: His realization that Gabby's secret-sharing tendency destroyed Hal
jokes.explanation: Good connecting of plot threads - Larry's detective work pays off with insight into Gabby's character.

jokes.text: She must've asked for a secret on their date... then told everybody about the mother-in-law.
jokes.setup: Larry figuring out why Hal is angry at him
jokes.punchline: Connecting Gabby's secret-sharing to Hal's public humiliation
jokes.explanation: Good detective work by Larry connecting the episode's various secret-sharing incidents.

jokes.text: Larry's umbrella defense: 'It holds up in gale-force winds. It never goes inside out. It's the best umbrella I ever had.' 'No umbrella like that.' 'This umbrella is like that.'
jokes.setup: Larry asks Mr. Takahashi to help retrieve his umbrella
jokes.punchline: His passionate defense of the umbrella's superior qualities
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's obsession with functional objects. His passion for umbrella engineering is endearing and absurd.

jokes.text: Larry's tuxedo philosophy: 'It's not the dressing up part that's disturbing to me, it's where you go after you're dressed up... Like, if I just sat in the house with a tuxedo on, that would be okay.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about formal wear
jokes.punchline: Larry's distinction between clothing and social obligations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insight into social anxiety - it's not the formal wear, it's the formal events. Very relatable and specific to Larry.

jokes.text: Larry's conditional romance: 'You know what? Even though... you gabbed and gave away a precious secret... I'm still considering having sex with you. I'm not taking it off the table.'
jokes.setup: Gabby has violated Larry's trust
jokes.punchline: His magnanimous offer to still potentially sleep with her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry arrogance - treating sex as a reward he might grant despite her failures. 'Not taking it off the table' is perfectly clinical.

jokes.text: The final restaurant reveal: 'He steal umbrella from Dallas Omni Hotel! She slept with student! He ask her to prom! He was captain of basketball team! She blew him in car!'
jokes.setup: Larry and Gabby return to the restaurant
jokes.punchline: All their secrets are revealed publicly with increasing detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect climactic revelation - all the episode's secrets come out at once in escalating detail. Great payoff structure.

jokes.text: The final shot of Larry's umbrella in the trash with 'Dallas Omni Hotel' visible
jokes.setup: The entire umbrella conflict throughout the episode
jokes.punchline: The umbrella he fought so hard for is discarded as trash
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual punchline - the object of Larry's obsession ends up as garbage. Great final irony.
